entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop, scientifically known as Cajanus cajan (L). Millsp., which has faced yield stagnation for over seven decades.

entities.name: SEED YIELD
entities.description: The amount of seed produced by the crop, a key trait that breeders aim to enhance in pigeonpea.

entities.name: YIELD STAGNATION
entities.description: A condition where the seed yield of pigeonpea has failed to increase over a long period.

entities.name: PURE LINE BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding method that was exclusively used for pigeonpea but proved ineffective in increasing yield.

entities.name: NATURAL OUT-CROSSING
entities.description: The biological process of cross-pollination in pigeonpea, which was initially ignored in breeding programs.

entities.name: HYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: A breeding programme initiated jointly by ICRISAT and ICAR to improve pigeonpea.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: An international research organization that collaborated with ICAR to initiate hybrid breeding in pigeonpea.

entities.name: ICAR
entities.description: An agricultural research council that collaborated with ICRISAT to initiate hybrid breeding in pigeonpea.

entities.name: POPULATION BREEDING
entities.description: A set of breeding schemes suggested and tried on a limited scale to improve pigeonpea.

entities.name: STRATIFIED MASS SELECTION
entities.description: A specific population breeding method with a strong technical base for enhancing seed yield in pigeonpea.

entities.name: SYBRID BREEDING
entities.description: A specific population breeding method with a strong technical base for enhancing seed yield in pigeonpea.
